...
“Physics AI is just glorified curve fitting.”

 That’s what an engineer told me last week.
...
Modern Physics AI models can map from boundary conditions and geometry to entire fields, so they’re clearly much more than simple regression. But I thought it’s actually a helpful mental model. These models do depend on training data, and extrapolation can fail.
...
Yet that doesn’t change
...
fact that a good “curve fit” can be incredibly useful in engineering - especially when exploring large design spaces for high-value problems where brute-force simulation would not be economical.
...
And the bar keeps moving. Model architectures are evolving: they generalize better, train faster, and are pushing the boundaries of where Physics AI is genuinely practical.
...
But that isn’t the full picture of how AI impacts product development and simulation. A huge amount of time is still lost in manual hand-offs, waiting for sim tasks to bubble up in the backlog, setting up models, cleaning geometry, rerunning variations, and writing up results. None of this adds real engineering value, yet it eats up bandwidth.
...
That’s where Engineering AI plays a role by automating the routine and standardizing best practices.
...

Provide engineering leadership across design, assembly, systems, and manufacturability to support product performance and repair quality. Support troubleshooting of technical issues, including nonconformance resolution, root cause analysis, corrective action development, and risk mitigation. Prepare technical documents (reports, procedures, etc.) and recommendations, apply advanced engineering analysis, and mentor engineers while driving continuous improvement and long-term capability building. Required Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Chemical or similar). Minimum of 5 years of experience in repair of rotating equipment or turbomachinery. Desired Characteristics Advanced degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related field. Knowledge of manufacturing and repair processes, including machining, welding/brazing, NDT, heat treat, metrology, assembly, and drafting/modeling. Proven ability to troubleshoot technical issues, resolve nonconformances, perform root cause analysis, and communicate effectively across technical and operational teams. Familiarity in repair engineering, mechanical design, manufacturing engineering, or related technical discipline. Experience in servicing, manufacturing, or operation of power generation, industrial equipment, or large rotating machinery. Experience developing engineering standards, repair procedures, or shop processes. Working knowledge of fatigue, fracture mechanics, thermal analysis, and mechanical integrity assessment. Familiarity of experience working with industry codes and standards (example ASME BPVC, AWS, API, NAVSEA, etc.). Demonstrated ability to influence across multiple business functions. Strong oral and written communication skills. GE Vernova offers a great work environment, professional development, challenging careers, and competitive compensation. results.title: GE Vernova's gas turbines power AI data center boom, orders full through 2031
highlights:
GE Vernova's gas turbines power AI data center boom, orders full through 2031
...
# GE Vernova’s gas turbines power AI data center boom, orders full through 2031
...
There’s a bottleneck forming in the AI revolution, and it has nothing to do with chips. It’s about electricity. GE Vernova’s heavy-duty gas turbines, the industrial workhorses that can deliver reliable, large-scale power to data centers, are completely sold out through 2030-2031, with lead times stretching to roughly three years.
...
The company’s Q1 2026 numbers tell the story in sharp relief. Total orders surged 71%, reaching $18.3 billion for the quarter. The electrification segment alone booked a record $2.4 billion in data center equipment orders in Q1 2026, a figure that surpassed total orders for the entire year of 2025. In one quarter, GE Vernova outdid an entire year.
...
## AI’s insatiable appetite for power
...
AI and data center load now constitutes approximately 20% of GE Vernova’s gas turbine backlog. That’s a fifth of one of the world’s largest turbine manufacturers’ pipeline dedicated to keeping servers humming.
...
A landmark deal in July 2025 illustrates the scale of what’s happening. Crusoe, an energy-focused data center company, ordered 29 LM2500XPRESS turbine units, representing roughly 1 GW of capacity, specifically for AI data center applications. That deal is part of a broader Texas-based project involving Microsoft and Chevron that’s expected to deliver approximately 2.7 GW of power using GE turbines, with first power anticipated in 2028.
...
Gas turbine pricing in the first half of 2026 is running 10 to 20 points higher compared to Q4 2025. Over a three-year window, some orders have seen price increases of up to 300%.
